data.body: PASS 1 review — criterion T1 (prior-art / novel-architecture): PASS, no findings. The plan crosses the external-integration bright-line (Jira round-trip), so T1 applies, but the plan affirmatively grounds itself in existing prior art rather than reinventing: (a) it explicitly reuses story A's FakeAcliClient/fixtures through the production fetcher path (tests/integration/rebar_reconciler/jira_contract/_fakes.py names this as "the foundation … the hermetic round-trip probe (story F) build on"); (b) the hermetic semantic-round-trip + anti-change-detector pattern is already established in tests/integration/rebar_reconciler/jira_contract/test_snapshot_contract.py and docs/adr/0004-reconciler-snapshot-contract.md, which this plan follows rather than inventing; (c) the "opt-in/scheduled live variant kept off the default suite" mirrors the existing tests/external/test_link_sync_roundtrip_live.py pattern; (d) the NOT-SYNCED guard to de-encode is a real, located artifact (scripts/jira-pressure-test/e2e_field_validation_probe.sh:1547,1914). No novel architectural pattern is introduced and no unverified capability assertion is made. No T1 finding.
